fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
The ICC Cricket World Cup was first held in 1975 in England. The West Indies won the inaugural tournament, defeating Australia in the final at Lord's.
India won the 1983 Cricket World Cup under the captaincy of Kapil Dev, defeating the West Indies in the final at Lord's, a result considered one of the greatest upsets in the sport's history.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, and 2002.
The UEFA Champions League, established in 1955 as the European Cup, is the most prestigious club football competition in the world.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Badminton has been an official Olympic sport since the 1992 Barcelona Games.
The Wimbledon Championships, first held in 1877, is the oldest tennis tournament in the world and the only Grand Slam still played on grass.
The four Grand Slam tournaments are the Australian Open, the French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open.
The NBA was founded in 1946 as the Basketball Association of America (BAA), and adopted the name National Basketball Association in 1949.
The Boston Celtics and the Los Angeles Lakers are tied for the most NBA championships, with 17 titles each as of the 2023-24 season.
